Definition.text: Reservation is a skill cost mode that reserves resource for as long as the skill effect is active.

Purpose.text: Prevent free stacking of simultaneously active resource-consuming effects by limiting available unreserved Mana/Life and applying opportunity-cost tradeoffs.

Core Rules.text: Base reservation can be an amount of Mana, Life, or both; many modifiers to Mana reservation now apply to Reservation for either resource. Reservation modifiers can be converted into Reservation Efficiency families (e.g., reduced Reservation replaced by increased Mana Reservation Efficiency).

Internal Rules.text: Reservation values are computed with decimal precision internally; the total reserved amount must be a whole number and never lower than 1. Skills can have both cost and reservation, with both conditions required to activate.

Calculation Order.text: 1) evaluate base reservation requirement, 2) apply changed reservation model and modifiers, 3) apply Reservation Efficiency as division of cost, 4) apply minimum/rounding constraints to final reserved amount, 5) enforce available unreserved resource check.

Priority Rules.text: Reservation Efficiency explicitly changes how reduction/efficiency modifiers apply; direct reduced reservation modifiers are replaced by efficiency forms in the relevant patch systems.

Preconditions.text: Character must have enough unreserved resource for the required reservation amount after all modifiers are applied.

Postconditions.text: Reserved amount remains unavailable while the reservation effect is active, and can persist through death only where the patch state indicates.

Limitations.text: Not all effects use reservation; some are explicitly changed to non-reservation skills in specific patch updates.

Caps.text: Cannot reserve less than 1 of the target resource due to whole-number resource constraints.

Formula.text: reservation_after_efficiency = base_reservation / (1 + reservation_efficiency_pct / 100); 100% increased Reservation Efficiency halves cost, and final reserved amount is clamped to whole-resource allocation constraints.

Conflicts.text: Pre-3.16 reduced Reservation behavior is superseded by Reservation Efficiency in that patch.

Exceptions.text: Mine-type skills are explicitly called out as able to have both cost and reservation, both needing fulfillment.

Hidden Rules.text: Decimal reservation percentages are stored with greater internal precision, while total reserved integer rounding can prevent small reductions from taking effect at low totals.

Developer Notes.text: Patch text frames Reservation reworks to address one-dimensional scaling and to make small investment meaningful while limiting extreme stacking.

Historical Changes.text: In 3.14, base reservation values and modifier behavior were broadened to support Mana/Life/dual-resource reservation and precision/rounding refinements; in 3.16, reduced Reservation was replaced by Reservation Efficiency.

text.note: Base reservation can be Mana, Life, or both; modifier scope expanded; percentage precision and whole-number reservation constraints were formalized; reservation no longer spends resource on activation.
Reservation Reduction replaced by Reservation Efficiency; efficiency is applied by dividing Reservation Effect cost; 100% increased efficiency halves reservation cost.
Reservation effects such as Heralds and Auras persist through death in at least listed patch behavior for Reservation effects.

text: 50% base reservation with 100% increased Reservation Efficiency reserves 25% under the 3.16 model.
A 50% reservation with +1% reduced reservation is represented as 49.5% internally; with 100 resource it still rounds to 50 reserved.
Reservation always consumes current Mana/Life on activation.
Resource reservation modifiers are always resource-specific even after 3.14 changes.
Reservation modifiers are purely additive percentage reductions independent of patch-specific efficiency rules.
